text: oh so THAT is why my timeline looks like a graveyard after three days of work
the real pro move is using symlinks for assets if you work across different drives
wait people actually organize their root folders?
i just name everything final_v2_real_final.html and hope for the best
that only works until you have to move a project to a different machine and half your links break because they're pointing to your desktop
ok but what about relative vs absolute paths? whenever i try to clean up the folder structure everything breaks
always relative. if you're using absolute paths you're just asking for pain when you switch from dev to production
the pain is part of the creative process
i swear i spend more time managing file directories than actually finishing the compositions
